title: Cut Flower Delivery for People Who Actually Care About Flowers
intro: For when you want delivered flowers that don't look like a grocery store impulse buy — sourced from growers who prioritize stem quality, variety selection, and vase life over flashy packaging.

items.title: Harmony Harvest Farm — Seasonal Cut Flower Bouquets
items.note: A working farm shipping direct — the kind of operation flower enthusiasts recommend when they want variety and stem quality over brand recognition.

items.title: PetalPickers — Farm Fresh Flowers
items.note: Overnight-shipped farm cuts at a price point that makes the middleman markup obvious by comparison.

items.title: Floom — Local Independent Florist Delivery
items.note: Routes orders to vetted independent florists rather than wire services — what people who care about who actually makes the arrangement reach for.

warnings: PetalPickers note on the product page states flower shipping resumes February 2025 — seasonality or dormancy needs human verification before publishing.
The Floral Source appears to be a wholesale/trade supplier, not retail — dropped; needs verification of public ordering availability.
Harmony Harvest Farm sourceUrl is the homepage, not a specific product page — no deeper product URL was available in the extracted data.
White Flower Farm cut flowers dropped: $180 price point and product mix (roses, lilies, orchids) skews toward gift-sender convenience, not enthusiast-grade sourcing.
Farmgirl Flowers, BloomNation, and UrbanStems dropped: mainstream positioning and affiliate-circuit ubiquity make them weak fits for this lens.
Debug mode: shortlist kept minimal to validate section structure and lens application — expand with additional farm-direct or specialty grower picks before publishing.
